resources.string: No KernelSU driver detected on your kernel, wrong kernel?

The images of the files with anime character sticker are copyrighted by %1$s, the Brand Intellectual Property in the images is owned by %2$s. Before using these files, in addition to complying with %3$s, you also need to comply with the authorization of the two authors to use these artistic contents.
Check for source code on GitHub
View third-party open-source libraries and their licenses
Are you sure you want to uninstall module %s?
Are you sure you want to uninstall module %s? This action will affect all modules, and certain features provided by the metamodule (such as mounting) will no longer work
Jailbreak may have failed, please check logs

You are in **Jailbreak mode**. Flashing a partition on a device with a **locked bootloader** will break AVB (Android Verified Boot) and may cause the device to **fail to boot**.\n\nMake sure your bootloader is unlocked before proceeding!
Automatically use Magica to escalate privileges when Permissive SELinux is detected at boot. Requires granting autostart permission to this app.
Run adbd daemon with root privileges
Prevent applications from detecting SELinux modifications
Modules are unavailable due to a conflict with Magisk!
Learn how to install KernelSU and use modules
KernelSU is, and always will be, free, and open source. You can however show us that you care by making a donation
Failed to update App Profile for %s
"You are using DEBUG build of manager, if you don't know what is DEBUG version, switch into RELEASE build immediately"
"The manager you are using is not the official %s manager, and you may not be able to obtain permissions."
Detected APatch, Everything maybe stop working.
The global default value for \"Umount modules\" in App Profile. If enabled, it will remove all module modifications to the system for apps that don\'t have a profile set
Enabling this option will allow KernelSU to restore any modified files by the modules for this app
New version %s is available, click to upgrade
Failed to update SELinux rules for %s
Manage local and online template of App Profile
Cannot find local template to export!
Auto-check for module and manager updates
This is a PR debug build. Do NOT use it in production!
The kernel was built with PR signature support. This is not a production kernel!
Select a image that needs to be patched
Your device will be **FORCED** to boot to the current inactive slot after a reboot!\nOnly use this option after OTA is done.\nContinue?
Temporarily uninstall KernelSU, restore to original state after next reboot
Uninstalling KernelSU (root and all modules) completely and permanently
Restore the stock factory image (if a backup exists), usually used before OTA; if you need to uninstall KernelSU, please use \"Uninstall permanently\"
This operation will overwrite the current allowlist. Continue?
Enable blur process for this app
Experiment feature, process with your own risk
Extract dynamic color seed color from custom background
Granting superuser to %s is not allowed
Allow root access via /system/bin/su, in new processes.
Unmount modules from kernel in App Profile
Kernel does not support this feature
Record root-related events into KernelSU sulog files.
This feature is managed by a module
Hides unnecessary cards when turned on
Hides Red dot about the number of super users, modules and KPM modules on the navigation bar page
Hide SuSFS status information on the home page
Hide Zygisk implementation information on the home page
Hide Meta Module implementation information on the home page
Hide link card information on the home page
Hide folder name,size,metamodule notice labels in module cards
No installed KernelPatch Modules at this time
The following kernel module functions were developed by KernelPatch and modified to include the kernel module functions of SukiSU Ultra

Please select: %1\$s Module Installation Mode 

Load: Temporarily load the module 
Embedded: Permanently install into the system
Unable to check if module file exists
Incorrect file type! Please select .kpm file
The following KPM will be uninstalled: %s
Please select the target slot for flashing boot
Select the animation effect for predictive back
Select the exit direction for predictive back
Change the launcher icon to KernelSU\'s icon
Hide KPM information and Function in home and bottom bar
Module %s is disabled, or pending removal
Adjust the screen display density for the current application only
Are you sure you want to change the application DPI from %1$d to %2$d?
%d Failed to install a new module
This module want to mount /system, meta module will handle that. Otherwise, it might not work
This feature allows you to customize the SuSFS uname value and build time spoofing. Enter the values you want to set and click Apply to take effect
Please enter build time spoofing value
SuSFS uname and build time set successfully: %s, %s
Automatically apply all non-default configurations on reboot
Configuration needs to be added to enable
No available configuration for auto start
This will clear all SUS path configurations. Are you sure you want to continue?
Display current SuSFS enabled features status
Enable or disable logging for SuSFS
Update JSON URL copied to clipboard
Display additional module information like update JSON URLs
Execute after file system is mounted but before system is fully booted，May cause a boot loop
View current boot slot information and copy values
SuSFS auto-start module enabled, module path: %s
Hint: You can use "default" to use the original value
Are you sure you want to clear all Kstat configurations? This action cannot be undone
• add_sus_kstat_statically: Static stat info of files/directories
• add_sus_kstat: Add path before bind mount, storing original stat info
• update_sus_kstat: Update target ino, keep size and blocks unchanged
• update_sus_kstat_full_clone: Update ino only, keep other original values
No Kstat configuration yet, click the button below to add
Control the hiding behavior of SUS mounts for processes
Hide SUS mounts for all processes
When enabled, SUS mounts will be hidden from all processes, including KSU processes
When disabled, SUS mounts will only be hidden from non-KSU processes, KSU processes can see the mounts
Enabled hiding SUS mounts for all processes
Disabled hiding SUS mounts for all processes

It is recommended to set to disabled after screen is unlocked, or during service.sh or boot-completed.sh stage, as this should fix the issue on some rooted apps that rely on mounts mounted by KSU process
Android Data path has been set to: %s
SD card path has been set to: %s
Path setup may not be fully successful, but SUS paths will continue to be added
Create a backup of all SuSFS configurations. The backup file will include all settings, paths, and configurations
Backup version mismatch, but will attempt to restore
Restore SuSFS configurations from a backup file. This will overwrite all current settings
Configuration restored successfully from backup created on %s from device: %s
This will overwrite all current SuSFS configurations. Are you sure you want to continue?
Overwrite bootloader locking status attribute in late_start service mode
Clean up the residual files and directories of various modules and tools (may be deleted by mistake, resulting in loss and failure to start, use with caution)
SuSFS library version mismatch, kernel: %1$s vs manager: %2$s, It is recommended to update the kernel or manager
Failed to set dynamic Manager configuration
Are you sure you want to clear all SUS loop paths? This action cannot be undone
Note: Only paths NOT inside /storage/ and /sdcard/ can be added via loop paths
Error: Loop paths cannot be inside /storage/ or /sdcard/ directories

Loop paths are re-flagged as SUS_PATH on each non-root user app or isolated service startup. This helps address issues where added paths may have their inode status reset or inode re-created in the kernel
AVC log spoofing has been enabled
AVC log spoofing has been disabled
Disabled: Disable spoofing the sus tcontext of \'su\' shown in avc log in kernel\n Enabled: Enable spoofing the sus tcontext of \'su\' with \'u:r:priv_app:s0:c512,c768\' shown in avc log in kernel

Important Note:\n - It is set to \'0\' by default in kernel\n - Enabling this will sometimes make developers hard to identify the cause when they are debugging with some permission or SELinux issue, so users are advised to disable this when doing
Apply KPM patch to kernel image before flashing
Use kernel as-is without any KPM modifications
This will remove all configured SUS maps. This action cannot be undone
Hide the mmapped real file from various maps in /proc/self/

Hide the real file paths of memory mappings from /proc/self/[maps|smaps|smaps_rollup|map_files|mem|pagemap]. Please note: This feature does not support hiding anonymous memory mappings, nor can it hide inline hooks or PLT hooks caused by the injected library itself
Important Notice: For applications with well-implemented injection detection mechanisms, this feature may not effectively bypass detection

First, find the target application\'s PID and UID using ps -enf, then check the relevant paths in /proc/<pid>/maps and compare the device numbers with those in /proc/1/mountinfo to ensure consistency. Only when the device numbers match can the map hiding function work properly
The current manager is incompatible with this kernel! Please upgrade the kernel to version %2$d or higher (currently %1$d)
Are you sure you want to delete the path %s?
All changes will take effect immediately.
module not mounted, because metamodule disabled
module not mounted, because metamodule being delete
module not mounted, because metamodule not installed
Please check your internet connection, and try again
Launcher does not support desktop shortcuts.
Please enable \"Create desktop shortcuts\" permission for this app in Xiaomi settings.
Please enable \"Desktop shortcut\" permission for this app in OPPO settings.
If shortcut creation fails, please enable desktop shortcut permission for this app in system settings.
Need Notification permission to show download progress.
Download failed: Notification permission is required
Need External Storage permission to write file.
Download failed: External Storage permission is required
